frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: value coding via divisive scaling of options against available alternatives

frameworks.name: cortical normalization (sensory analogy)
frameworks.one_line: canonical sensory computation extended to reward valuation in OFC

frameworks.name: economic decision making / value-based choice
frameworks.one_line: context-dependent valuation underlying choice between meaningful alternatives

core_question: Do mOFC value signals follow divisive normalization, and does behavioral task context modulate that normalization?
key_assumptions: value can be represented by single-neuron firing in mOFC
divisive normalization is a candidate computation for value coding
task context can alter how alternatives enter the normalization pool
manipulations: free choice between two reward-yielding lottery options
forced choice where one option is certain not to yield reward
key_findings: mOFC neurons signal relative option values via a divisive normalization function under free choice
the normalization model fits poorly under forced choice with one non-rewarding option
value normalization in mOFC is context-specific
interpretations: mOFC contributes valuation signals critical for economic decision making when meaningful alternatives are available
normalization of reward value depends on whether alternatives are behaviorally meaningful
